title: My Rest Is Not Laziness. It Is Necessary.
subtitle: An affirmation for women who cannot stop without guilt
excerpt: A direct affirmation for the persistent internal voice that names any pause as laziness. Says the plain thing: rest is necessary, not earned and not a failure of productivity.
sourceNotes: Written for homemade.education, drawing on the rest-as-resistance tradition in women's health writing.

practice.whenToUse: Use when you have sat down, or need to sit down, and the internal voice is already calling it laziness.

content.text: A short affirmation for the moment you stop, and the voice that immediately names that stopping as a failure. Say it before the guilt gets loud.
Say it aloud or in your head. Three times. The period in the middle is deliberate. Two complete sentences, not one hedged one.
My rest is not laziness. It is necessary.
Then sit with the rest. You do not have to justify it. The affirmation is not asking you to earn it. It is asking you to receive it without apology.
Written for homemade.education. Draws on the rest-as-resistance tradition in feminist health writing, which positions rest as a political and biological necessity rather than a reward to be earned.
